title: Officina Stellare wins $2 million contract for lasercom ground station in Spain
summary: Officina Stellare secured a $2 million contract to build a laser communication ground station in Spain for ICFO. The project aims to support future space-to-Earth laser and quantum-encrypted communications.

title: Rocket Lab launches eighth Synspective radar imaging satellite
summary: Rocket Lab successfully launched the eighth radar imaging satellite for Synspective, a Japanese company specializing in Earth observation. This mission marks another step in expanding Synspective's synthetic aperture radar (SAR) satellite constellation.

title: Another GPS launch shifts from ULA to SpaceX as Vulcan investigation continues
summary: A GPS III satellite launch has been reassigned from ULA's Vulcan Centaur to SpaceX due to ongoing investigations into the Vulcan rocket. This marks another shift in launch providers amid delays in the Vulcan program.

title: OHB Sweden wins €248 million contract to build EPS-Sterna constellation
summary: OHB Sweden secured a €248 million contract to build 20 satellites for the EPS-Sterna constellation, enhancing Europe's weather forecasting and climate monitoring. This marks the largest deal in Sweden's space sector.

title: Holistic space observation: the shift from SSA to SDA
summary: SpaceX's proposal to deploy up to one million satellites in low Earth orbit, combined with AI-enabled autonomous orbital infrastructure, signals a major shift toward large-scale, strategic space operations. This move highlights the transition from Space Situational Awareness (SSA) to Space Domain Awareness (SDA) in the space industry.

title: ESA to fly dedicated Crew Dragon mission to ISS
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will charter a SpaceX Crew Dragon mission to the International Space Station (ISS) to provide additional flight opportunities for its astronauts.

title: Kayhan targets investors, insurers with expanded orbital intelligence platform
summary: Kayhan Space is expanding its orbital intelligence platform to offer business insights for investors and insurers, moving beyond satellite fleet coordination. The new software leverages existing data to cater to financial stakeholders.

title: Blue Origin joins the orbital data center race
summary: Blue Origin has entered the orbital data center market by proposing a constellation of up to 51,600 satellites. This move positions the company to compete in the growing space-based data infrastructure sector.

title: Register now: The energy imperative driving the push toward orbital data centers
summary: A virtual event on March 31, sponsored by Star Catcher and the Commercial Space Federation, will discuss the energy-driven push for orbital data centers. The event highlights the growing interest in space-based computing infrastructure.

title: Kratos wins $446 million Space Force contract for missile-tracking ground systems
summary: Kratos secured a $446 million contract from the U.S. Space Force to develop ground management and integration systems for missile-tracking satellites under the Resilient Missile Warning and Tracking program.

title: Astronomers keep finding new moons of Jupiter and Saturn
summary: Astronomers have discovered four new moons orbiting Jupiter and 11 new moons around Saturn, expanding the known celestial bodies in our solar system.

title: US Space Force moves GPS launch to SpaceX Falcon 9 due to Vulcan rocket glitch
summary: The U.S. Space Force has switched from ULA's Vulcan Centaur to SpaceX's Falcon 9 for an upcoming GPS satellite launch due to an investigation into a rocket anomaly. The decision highlights ongoing reliability concerns with the Vulcan rocket.

title: Love Project Hail Mary? Here are the Andy Weir books you need to read
summary: The article recommends books by Andy Weir for fans of 'Project Hail Mary', including 'The Martian' and 'Artemis'. It highlights Weir's popular sci-fi works.

title: 'Project Hail Mary' is an optimistic look towards the stars, and we need that right now (review)
summary: The article reviews 'Project Hail Mary,' presenting an optimistic perspective on space exploration. It highlights the need for such positive outlooks in current times.

title: Hubble Space Telescope accidentally witnesses comet C/2025 K1 (ATLAS) breaking apart
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured a rare cosmic event where comet C/2025 K1 (ATLAS) broke apart in real time. This accidental observation provides new insights into comet fragmentation processes.

title: Northern lights may be visible in 18 states tonight and over the weekend
summary: Auroras may be visible in 18 U.S. states, including Alaska and Illinois, due to coronal mass ejections and solar wind impacting Earth. The Northern Lights could be seen tonight and over the weekend.

title: Rocket Lab scores $190 million launch deal to test hypersonic tech for US military
summary: Rocket Lab secured a $190 million contract with the U.S. Department of Defense for 20 launches of its suborbital HASTE rocket to test hypersonic technology. This deal marks a significant expansion of Rocket Lab's role in supporting U.S. military hypersonic research.

title: Artemis 2 moon rocket rolls back to launch pad | Space photo of the day for March 20, 2026
summary: NASA's Space Launch System rocket, part of the Artemis 2 mission, rolled back to the launch pad ahead of its second moon mission, marking the first crewed flight of the vehicle. The mission aims to advance lunar exploration efforts.

title: NASA's Artemis 2 moon rocket arrives back at the launch pad
summary: NASA's Space Launch System (SLS) rocket has returned to the launch pad in preparation for the Artemis 2 mission, which aims to send astronauts around the moon next month.

title: NASA’s X-59 Experimental Supersonic Aircraft Makes Second Flight
summary: NASA's X-59 experimental supersonic aircraft completed its second test flight, marking the start of numerous planned flights ahead of 2026. The abbreviated flight, due to a technical issue, still provided valuable data while ensuring safe landing.

title: Hangar One Restoration Project
summary: Restoration of Hangar One, a historic aviation landmark in the San Francisco Bay Area, has been completed. The project was finished by Planetary Ventures in December 2025.

title: NASA Selects University Finalists for Technology Concepts Competition
summary: NASA selected 14 university teams as finalists for the 2026 RASC-AL Competition, challenging them to design innovative concepts for human exploration on the Moon, Mars, and beyond. The competition aims to foster innovation by linking academia with the aerospace community.

title: How Open NASA Data on Comet 3I/ATLAS Will Power Tomorrow’s Discoveries
summary: NASA has collected extensive data on the interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS, which is leaving our solar system. The public data archives will enable future scientific discoveries.

title: Smiles and Spacesuits
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams undergoes spacesuit fit verification inside the ISS's Quest airlock to ensure airtightness and mobility. The procedure also assesses comfort and mitigates safety risks.

title: NASA Exploration, Science Inspire “Project Hail Mary” Film
summary: NASA's upcoming Artemis II mission, the first crewed flight under the Artemis program, aligns with the premiere of the sci-fi film 'Project Hail Mary,' blending real-life space exploration with cinematic science fiction.

title: NASA Simulations Improve Artemis II Launch Environment
summary: NASA used advanced simulations to optimize airflow around the Artemis II Space Launch System (SLS) rocket during its test flight around the Moon. The simulations aimed to ensure the best possible launch conditions by analyzing the rocket's flight environment.

title: NASA Glenn Opens Applications for Free Summer Engineering Institute
summary: NASA Glenn Research Center is accepting applications for its free 2026 High School Engineering Institute, offering hands-on aerospace training for rising juniors and seniors. Applications open March 20 and close May 1.

title: Restless Kīlauea Launches Lava and Ash
summary: Kīlauea volcano's ongoing eruption reached its 43rd episode, characterized by intense lava fountains and significant ash dispersal. This event highlights the volcano's persistent activity and its impact on the surrounding environment.

title: American Bald Eagle at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center
summary: NASA's Kennedy Space Center in Florida conducted annual bald eagle nesting surveys on March 13, 2026, documenting active and inactive nests across the facility and nearby wildlife refuges.

title: SLS enters pad flow ahead of historic Artemis II mission
summary: NASA's Space Launch System (SLS) rocket and Orion spacecraft have arrived at the launch pad in preparation for the historic Artemis II mission. This marks a significant milestone ahead of the crewed lunar flyby mission.

title: Booster 19 concludes initial test campaign on Pad 2
summary: Booster 19 has completed its initial test campaign on Pad 2, marking another milestone for SpaceX's Starship Flight 12 mission.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Spectrum, Electron to launch during busy week of launches
summary: Nine launches are scheduled for the week, including SpaceX's Falcon 9, Rocket Lab's Electron, and Isar Aerospace's Spectrum. The launches span multiple countries and organizations.

title: Blue Origin Unveils NEO Hunter: A Hybrid Planetary Defense Concept
summary: Blue Origin has introduced NEO Hunter, a new mission concept aimed at planetary defense. The hybrid system is designed to detect and potentially deflect near-Earth objects.

title: From Steel Rolls to Starship at the Starfactory
summary: SpaceX is accelerating its plans to send humans to the Moon and Mars, transforming its Starfactory facility to support Starship production. The company is repurposing the facility from steel rolls to spacecraft manufacturing.

title: New crew arrivals and science highlight February aboard the ISS
summary: February aboard the ISS was marked by new crew arrivals and scientific activities following the early departure of Crew-11 in mid-January. The International Space Station continued its operations with a focus on research and crew transitions.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to dominate another busy week of missions
summary: Eight launches are scheduled worldwide this week, with SpaceX's Falcon 9 dominating the launch schedule. The upcoming missions highlight Falcon 9's continued prominence in the space industry.

title: SpaceX’s Enters a new era with Booster 19 Testing on Pad 2
summary: SpaceX has begun testing Booster 19 on Pad 2, marking a new phase in operations since Flight 11 on October 13, 2025. This follows a 147-day gap since the last mission.

title: Firefly Alpha FLTA007 “Stairway to Seven” launches successfully
summary: Firefly Aerospace successfully launched its Alpha FLTA007 rocket, codenamed 'Stairway to Seven,' marking its return to flight after a nearly 10-month pause. The mission was highlighted as a significant step for the company's commercial launch capabilities.

title: Baikonur Launch Pad restored, clearing path for Progress MS-33 ISS Mission
summary: Russia's Baikonur Cosmodrome's Site 31/6 launch pad has been fully restored, enabling the upcoming Progress MS-33 mission to the International Space Station (ISS). The repair work clears the path for critical resupply operations.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website has been mentioned as a source for space industry news, but no specific details about the news content are provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. This commemorative publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long anomaly. The mission team reports that the spacecraft is now operational and communicating again.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting a series of events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25), detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities across ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to hunt for Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is set for a January 2027 launch on an Ariane 6 rocket.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA will release ECOS version 6 on 30 March 2026, requiring all suppliers to use it for financial proposals in ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher developed a 3D-printed, football-sized lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference while being cost-effective and simple to manufacture.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly on the Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have begun conducting experiments on ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates low-gravity environments. This initiative aims to support research and hands-on learning in space technology.

title: Kayhan Space Launches Satcat Terminal
summary: Kayhan Space has launched a new Satcat Terminal business, which may appeal to liberal arts majors. The initiative expands their offerings in the space industry.

title: Inside Austria’s Small But Mighty Space Ecosystem
summary: Austria's space industry is experiencing a renaissance with increased funding and startup activity. The country's small but growing ecosystem is gaining momentum.

title: Portal, Paladin Team On Debris Removal Service
summary: Portal and Paladin are collaborating on a debris removal service to make it operational rather than experimental. The initiative aims to maintain critical satellite infrastructure for communications, navigation, weather forecasting, and national security.

title: HyImpulse Signs Launch Agreement with SaxaVord
summary: HyImpulse has signed a launch agreement with SaxaVord for its second suborbital flight of the SR75 rocket, scheduled for no earlier than Q3 2026.

title: Apex Signs First Japanese Bus Contract With NEC
summary: Apex has signed its first satellite bus contract in Japan with NEC Corporation, which will purchase an Aries satellite bus for a communications demonstration mission scheduled for next year.

title: Modified Vulcan Expected to Launch This Summer
summary: The Vulcan rocket has undergone modifications to improve its nozzle and solid rocket booster, with its next launch expected this summer. These changes were planned before a recent anomaly and are now being accelerated.

title: Advanced Navigation Raises $110M Series C
summary: Advanced Navigation, an Australia-based company, raised $110 million in a Series C funding round to reduce reliance on satellite infrastructure. The funding will support the company's efforts to develop alternative navigation technologies.

title: NanoAvionics Lands Korean National Security Deal
summary: NanoAvionics secured a national security deal with South Korea, marking another step in its global expansion strategy. The partnership highlights the growing demand for space technology in South Korea's defense sector.

title: FAA Ends Part 450 Transition Period
summary: The FAA has concluded the transition period for Part 450 licensing requirements, with all US launchers now operating under the updated five-year-old regulations. This marks the end of legacy regulations for commercial space launches.

title: How Congress Can Grow the Space Supply Chain
summary: The space industry's supply chain is struggling to meet growing demand, but policymakers can help accelerate growth according to two industry groups.

title: Spire Targets 50% Growth in 2026 After Adjustments in 2025
summary: Spire Global aims for 50% revenue growth in 2026 after restructuring in 2025, including divesting its maritime business and reducing debt. CEO Theresa Condor outlined these plans to investors.

title: Eutelsat CEO Fallacher Talks OneWeb Gen 2, Global Competition, and Sovereign Demand
summary: Eutelsat Group CEO Jean-François Fallacher discusses the company's strategic focus on OneWeb Gen 2, global competition, and sovereign demand amid its ongoing transformation. The interview highlights Eutelsat's evolving role in the space industry.

title: Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space Plan Repeatable Debris Removal Missions
summary: Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space are collaborating to provide commercial debris removal missions under a recurring service model called Debris Removal as a Service (DRAAS). This partnership aims to address the growing issue of space debris through regular, commercialized cleanup operations.
company: Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space

title: SLI Purchases Two Small GEO Satellites From ReOrbit
summary: SLI has agreed to purchase two small Geostationary Orbit (GEO) communication satellites from ReOrbit for 150 million euros ($172.8 million). The deal was announced in a joint statement by both companies.

title: Comtech Delivers First Set of Digital Modems to US Army
summary: Comtech delivered the first set of Enterprise Digital Intermediate Frequency Multi-Carrier (EDIM) modems to the U.S. Army under a $48.6 million contract signed in 2023. This delivery marks a significant step in modernizing the Army's communication infrastructure.

title: Latest White House Cybersecurity Strategy Talks Crypto, AI, Quantum
summary: The White House released President Trump's Cyber Strategy for America, emphasizing securing critical infrastructure and maintaining leadership in emerging technologies like crypto, AI, and quantum. The strategy outlines six policy pillars to address cybersecurity challenges.

title: Getting Better Outcomes In Space: How Will the UK Embrace the Challenge?
summary: A panel at Space-Comm in London discussed the security of UK space assets, featuring perspectives from government and industry experts.

title: SpiderOak Wins New UAS Systems Contract
summary: SpiderOak has secured a new contract from the U.S. Army Unmanned Aircraft Systems (PMO UAS) to conduct supply chain analysis and cybersecurity assessments for multiple vendors. This contract highlights SpiderOak's role in enhancing security and reliability within the UAS ecosystem.

title: Ukraine Ambassador to UK Talks Future Warfare
summary: The Ukrainian ambassador to the UK discussed the future of warfare at an event in London, marking four years since Russia's invasion. Valerii Zaluzhnyi highlighted evolving military strategies and technological advancements.

title: Ligado Tells FCC That SkyTerra Next Proposal Won’t Cause Interference
summary: Ligado has filed a proposal with the FCC to host an L-band payload on AST SpaceMobile satellites, asserting that it won't cause interference. The company claims the plan aligns with existing L-band coordination.
